title: Root
description: How do we make pixelpack encode real programs efficiently? Current state: 3-254 byte programs encoded as multi-pixel PNGs. 254-byte fibonacci needs 56 pixels (1792 bits) which is terrible. Each pixel uses 1 of 16 strategies to expand a 4-bit seed + 28 data bits into a short byte string. Next leap: fractal unfold where pixels reference each other, better cross-strategy composition, or fundamentally different encoding approaches. What paths lead to the biggest capability gains?

children.title: Hierarchical Pixel Encoding
children.description: Introduce a tree-based hierarchy where pixels reference and compose with each other.
enables: Efficient encoding of large programs by reducing redundancy through shared components.
Facilitates modular design, allowing for easier updates and maintenance.
risk: Increased complexity in both encoding and decoding algorithms.
Potential performance overhead from managing hierarchical structures.

children.title: Hierarchical Program Decomposition
children.description: Pixels reference and compose with each other to create a tree-based hierarchy.
enables: Nested program structures, allowing for modular and reusable code components.
Dynamic program generation by combining different pixel configurations.
children.risk: Increased complexity in encoding and decoding processes. Potential for unintended interactions between pixels.

children.title: Parameterized Pixel Libraries
children.description: Create a library of parameterized pixels that can be combined to form complex programs.
enables: Standardization of program components, simplifying the creation and sharing of complex algorithms.
Community-driven development of pixel libraries for various applications.
children.risk: Over-reliance on pre-defined libraries could stifle creativity and innovation.

children.title: Pixel Evolution via Genetic Algorithms
children.description: Use genetic algorithms to evolve pixels into more efficient or effective program components.
enables: Automated optimization of programs through evolutionary processes.
Emergence of novel program structures that could outperform manually designed ones.
children.risk: Unpredictable outcomes due to the stochastic nature of genetic algorithms. Potential for slow convergence.

children.title: Multi-layered Pixel Encoding
children.description: Introduce multiple layers of pixel encoding, each layer adding a level of complexity and capability.
enables: Gradual increase in program complexity by stacking layers, allowing for fine-grained control over generated programs.
Specialized layers for different aspects of programming (e.g., logic, data handling, UI) to be encoded separately.
children.risk: Increased difficulty in managing and understanding multi-layered encodings. Potential for performance overheads.

children.title: Pixel-based Distributed Computing
children.description: Distribute the execution of pixel-encoded programs across a network of devices, enabling parallel processing.
enables: Scalable computation by leveraging distributed systems.
Enhanced fault tolerance and redundancy in program execution.
children.risk: Complexity in managing distributed environments. Potential for security vulnerabilities if not implemented carefully.

children.title: Fractal Unfold Strategy
children.description: Develop a strategy that enables pixels to reference other pixels, creating self-similar patterns of expansion.
enables: Significant reduction in pixel count needed for encoding large programs.
Supports highly efficient encoding and decoding by leveraging fractal properties.
risk: Challenges current architecture assumptions about isolated seed processing.
Requires a fundamental shift in how the system understands and processes expansion strategies.

children.title: Leverage Neural Network for Encoding
children.description: Use machine learning models to generate pixel sequences that encode complex programs with minimal overhead.
enables: Automated optimization of encoding efficiency, potentially surpassing manual methods.
Paves the way for advanced self-encoding systems capable of handling extremely complex algorithms.
risk: High initial investment in training and maintaining neural network models.
Potential loss of interpretability in the encoded programs, complicating debugging and analysis.
children.prune_reason: Duplicate of: Root: How do we make pixelpack encode real programs efficiently? Current state: 3-254 byte programs encode

children.title: Multi-Dimensional Pixel Encoding
children.description: Expand encoding beyond just color channels to include other dimensions like alpha transparency, pixel position, etc.
enables: Increased capacity of each pixel to encode information, allowing for more compact representations.
Facilitates advanced visual and interactive applications within the encoded programs.
risk: Requires significant overhaul of existing algorithms and data structures.
Possible compatibility issues with current systems expecting standard 32-bit RGBA pixels.

children.title: Integration with Quantum Computing
children.description: Explore the use of quantum computing to enhance encoding efficiency and capability, particularly for complex programs.
enables: Breakthroughs in scalability and complexity handling for encoded programs.
Puts pixelpack at the forefront of cutting-edge computational technologies.
risk: High barrier to entry due to quantum computing expertise and hardware requirements.
Potential for rapid obsolescence as quantum computing technology evolves rapidly.

children.title: Quantum-Based Encoding Strategy
children.description: Develop a new encoding strategy using quantum superposition to encode multiple seeds in a single pixel.
enables: Massive parallelism in program generation, exponentially increasing the number of possible programs from a single pixel.
Integration with quantum networks for secure and fast sharing of encoded programs.
risk: Dependence on quantum hardware, which is currently expensive and not universally accessible.
Potential complexity in maintaining backward compatibility with existing encoding strategies.

children.title: Quantum-Enhanced Auto-Growing Dictionary
children.description: Use quantum algorithms to dynamically generate and update the auto-growing dictionary based on real-time program data.
enables: Real-time adaptation of encoding strategies to optimize for specific program types or usage patterns.
Advanced machine learning techniques that leverage quantum computing's strengths in pattern recognition.
risk: High initial setup costs and potential for increased complexity in managing dictionary updates.
Reliance on quantum computing infrastructure, which may introduce new failure modes.

children.title: Quantum-Proofed Pixel Encoding
children.description: Design a pixel encoding scheme that remains secure and functional even under quantum attacks, such as Shor's algorithm.
enables: Enhanced security for encoded programs against potential future threats from quantum computers.
Trustworthy distribution of encoded software in environments where quantum capabilities are evolving.
risk: Complexity in maintaining and upgrading encoding schemes to adapt to new quantum vulnerabilities.
Potential reduction in the size of encodable programs due to security overheads.

children.title: Quantum-Assisted Segmentation
children.description: Implement a segmentation algorithm that uses quantum computing to identify optimal segment boundaries for complex programs.
enables: Significantly improved efficiency in encoding long and complex programs by optimizing segment size and content.
Reduction in the total number of pixels required, potentially leading to more compact encoded images.
risk: Necessity of quantum hardware and specialized software for segmentation, limiting accessibility.
Risk that current algorithms may not translate well into quantum environments, requiring extensive redesign.
children.prune_reason: Duplicate of: Integration with Quantum Computing

children.title: Quantum-Enabled Expansion Strategies
children.description: Create a set of expansion strategies specifically optimized for quantum computing environments to enhance the generation and execution of encoded programs.
enables: Leveraging quantum parallelism to speed up the expansion process, drastically reducing the time needed to decode and run complex programs.
New forms of computation within encoded programs that take full advantage of quantum properties.
risk: Increased complexity in strategy selection and implementation, requiring specialized expertise.
Potential for performance degradation if the expansion strategies are not well-suited to classical hardware.
children.prune_reason: Duplicate of: Integration with Quantum Computing
